policy_excerpts.policy_title: Aetna Oncology Medical Policy — PARP Inhibitors in Prostate Cancer
policy_excerpts.section_heading: Olaparib in mCRPC with HRR-mutated disease
policy_excerpts.excerpt_text: Olaparib is medically necessary for mCRPC patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic HRR-gene mutation, post-novel-hormonal-agent. Acceptable HRR genes include BRCA1/2, ATM, BARD1, BRIP1, CDK12, CHEK1/2, FANCL, PALB2, RAD51B/C/D, RAD54L.

policy_excerpts.policy_title: Aetna Oncology Medical Policy — PARP Inhibitors in Prostate Cancer
policy_excerpts.excerpt_text: VUS findings alone do not establish HRR-mutated disease. Clinician judgment + molecular tumor board recommendation may support coverage on case-by-case basis.

criteria.evidence: Stage IV mCRPC documented; PSA progression on castration-resistant therapy
ATM pathogenic variant detected (qualifies). BRCA1 VUS does not (per § Variants of Unknown Significance). Single-criterion assessment inconclusive without clinician review.

necessity_assessment.hitl_reason: ATM pathogenic variant alone qualifies under coverage criteria, but BRCA1 VUS in same panel introduces clinical interpretation question requiring oncology pharmacist or molecular tumor board judgment. Defer to clinician.
necessity_assessment.summary: Patient has clear ATM pathogenic mutation that qualifies. BRCA1 VUS introduces an interpretive question that exceeds the necessity reasoner's confidence threshold.

pause_reason: Necessity Reasoner overall_confidence 0.62 < HITL_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD (0.75). Routing to qualified clinician per CMS-0057-F § IV.C and CA SB 1120 — ATM mutation alone qualifies under § Olaparib in mCRPC, but BRCA1 VUS introduces an interpretive question. Awaiting clinician signoff via POST /api/v1/cases/DEMO-003/resume.

expected_body.reviewer_note: Clinical justification (will appear in audit trail)

reviewer_action_required.regulatory_context: CMS-0057-F § IV.C requires qualified-clinician review on adverse determinations; CA SB 1120 requires physician sign-off on AI-assisted decisions
